description: The Secondigliano Alliance is a strategic alliance of Camorra clans based in Naples, Italy, founded in the late 1980s by Gennaro Licciardi, Edoardo Contini, and Francesco Mallardo. It controls drug trafficking and extortion rackets in Naples and its hinterland, with branches in the Netherlands and Spain. Considered one of the two most powerful Camorra organizations, it has been led by Maria Licciardi and later Vincenzo Licciardi. Despite police crackdowns, it remains active.

country_links.evidence_quote: The Alliance is also believed to use Santo Domingo in the Dominican Republic to launder money.
country_links.context: The organization uses Santo Domingo in the Dominican Republic to launder money.

country_links.evidence_quote: The criminal group has branches in the Netherlands and in Spain, with interests in international drug trafficking and money laundering.
country_links.context: The organization has branches in the Netherlands and uses it as a node for drug trafficking and money laundering.

country_links.evidence_quote: The group is believed by the authorities to be allied with the Commisso 'ndrina in the trafficking and sale of cocaine and marijuana from South America, via the Netherlands.
country_links.context: The organization sources cocaine and marijuana from South America via the Netherlands.

country_links.evidence_quote: The criminal group has branches in the Netherlands and in Spain, with interests in international drug trafficking and money laundering.
country_links.context: The organization has branches in Spain, is strongly active there, and members have been arrested in Spain.
